summary: Matched and linked the Samus Bomb item unit by adding its item state table data, making small codegen-sensitive source adjustments in `itsamusbomb.c`, correcting a Samus bomb owner argument in `it_802B5478`, and promoting the object to `Matching` in `configure.py`. The decomp.dev report credited four new matches for `main/melee/it/items/itsamusbomb`: `.data`, `.sdata2`, `itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Phys`, and `it_802B5478`.
what_changed: `src/melee/it/items/itsamusbomb.c` added the global `ItemStateTable it_803F7220[]` with four Samus bomb motion entries, including animation, physics, and collision callbacks for motions 0 through 2 and an anim-only terminal entry for motion 3.
`itsamusbomb.c` moved `baselib/mtx.h` from a quoted include to an angle-bracket include after the local project includes.
`my_sqrtf` in `itsamusbomb.c` added a local `u8 _[4] = { 0 };` padding/stack-shape variable before the existing `volatile float y`, indicating a codegen/stack-layout matching tweak.
`itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Phys` moved `f32 mult` declaration from inside the speed clamp branch to function scope before the branch, another small codegen-sensitive adjustment.

`it_802B5478` changed the first argument passed to `ftSs_Init_80128A1C` from the bomb item gobj to `ip->xDD4_itemVar.samusbomb.owner`, matching the subsequent owner-based call to `ftSs_Init_80128944`.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` converted several former global autogenerated item symbols to local labels such as `@246`, `@244`, `@245`, `@165`, `@190`, `@243`, `@247`, `@324`, and `@325`, covering strings, floats, and doubles used by the matched unit.
`src/melee/it/items/itlikelike.c` only reordered `baselib/gobj.h` from quoted include style to angle-bracket include style; the diff shows no behavioral item logic change there.
smart_moves: Matched the whole item object instead of only individual functions: code edits were paired with `.data` and `.sdata2` symbol-localization changes so the unit could be linked.
Defined the Samus bomb `ItemStateTable` directly in C with existing motion callback names, allowing `.data` to become 100% matched according to the bot report.
Used minimal, targeted codegen nudges: a stack padding array in `my_sqrtf` and moving `mult` declaration scope were enough to close tiny remaining mismatches without broader rewrites.
Corrected `ftSs_Init_80128A1C` to receive the stored bomb owner gobj rather than the item gobj, which is both semantically consistent with the owner null-check and supported by the matching result.
Kept local constants and strings as local `@NNN` symbols in `symbols.txt` rather than preserving misleading global `it_...` names once the owning C unit could emit them.
decomp_lessons: For item units, matching may require reconstructing the `ItemStateTable` data alongside function bodies; data-section completion can be the blocker for switching an object to `Matching`.
Tiny stack-layout differences can matter: introducing an otherwise unused initialized local byte array before a volatile float changed `my_sqrtf` enough to support matching.

Declaration placement can affect register allocation or stack layout; moving a temporary such as `f32 mult` out of an inner block is a valid matching tactic when evidence comes from diff/compiler output.
When a function checks an item variable owner pointer immediately before a fighter/Samus helper call, verify whether the original code passed the owner gobj rather than the item gobj.
Once data is emitted from C, update `symbols.txt` scope from global `it_...` placeholders to local `@NNN` labels for compiler-generated local constants/strings.
naming_conventions: Samus bomb callbacks use `itSamusbomb_UnkMotionN_Anim`, `itSamusbomb_UnkMotionN_Phys`, and `itSamusbomb_UnkMotionN_Coll` naming.
The item state table retained the address-style name `it_803F7220`, matching the existing symbol address rather than introducing a descriptive name in this PR.
Local compiler-emitted data labels in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` use `@NNN` names with `scope:local`.
Item gobj, fighter gobj, and parent/owner conventions are visible as `Item_GObj*`, `Fighter_GObj*`, `parent_gobj`, and `ip->xDD4_itemVar.samusbomb.owner`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added `ItemStateTable it_803F7220[]` to reproduce `.data` layout for the item unit.
Changed anonymous/global symbols for floats, doubles, and strings in `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2` to local `@NNN` labels in `symbols.txt`.
Inserted `u8 _[4] = { 0 };` in `my_sqrtf` before `volatile float y` as an apparent stack-layout/codegen match tactic.
Moved `f32 mult` declaration to the top of `itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Phys` before the branch to influence generated code.
Adjusted include style/order for `baselib/mtx.h` and `baselib/gobj.h`; evidence shows this was part of the matching/linking diff, but no explicit rationale was documented.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

decomp.dev bot reported improved GALE01 progress: matched code 54.50% (+0.01%, +336 bytes), linked code 31.10% (+0.07%, +2640 bytes), matched data 34.52% (+0.01%, +136 bytes), and linked data 28.89% (+0.01%, +152 bytes).

decomp.dev bot listed four new matches for `main/melee/it/items/itsamusbomb`: `.data` +104 bytes from 0.00% to 100.00%, `.sdata2` +7 bytes from 80.00% to 100.00%, `itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Phys` +1 byte from 99.96% to 100.00%, and `it_802B5478` +1 byte from 99.63% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other item units that are nearly matched but still missing their `ItemStateTable` definitions.
Search for other calls to `ftSs_Init_80128A1C` to confirm expected first-argument conventions for Samus helper functions.
Audit remaining global `it_804D...` and `it_804DD...` constants in `symbols.txt` that may become local `@NNN` labels after item units are matched.
Look for other `my_sqrtf` implementations using `__frsqrte` and stack-padding locals for matching consistency.
